channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: CLSA Sees ONGC Stock Up 65%, OMCs May Delay Fuel Price Hikes
item.description: CLSA forecasts significant upside for Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC), predicting a 65% surge. The firm also suggests oil marketing companies (OMCs) like IOC, BPCL, and HPCL may hold off on substantial petrol and diesel price increases, even with crude oil near $100 per barrel. This is despite OMCs potentially needing to balance profits after recent high margins. However, Ambit Institutional Equities recommends selling OMC stocks due to balance sheet risks.

item.title: Bitcoin Near $70K as Global Turmoil Persists, Warning Signs Emerge
item.description: Bitcoin has shown remarkable resilience, holding near $70,000 despite geopolitical turmoil and shifting interest rate expectations. However, critical on-chain metrics, including a negative Coinbase Premium and decelerating Bitcoin ETF inflows, suggest underlying institutional demand may be weakening. This divergence presents a complex picture for investors.

item.title: Indian Office Leasing Surges 15%, Driven by Demand Despite Global Woes
item.description: India's office space leasing reached 18.3 million sq ft in Q1 2024, a 15% year-over-year increase, according to Colliers India. Strong occupier demand and the expansion of Global Capability Centres (GCCs) drove this growth, even as global economic uncertainties persisted. However, the market's performance varied sharply by city, with Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, and Mumbai showing significant gains, while Delhi-NCR and Chennai experienced notable declines.

item.title: India Housing: Luxury Homes Soar, Affordable Market Under Pressure
item.description: India's housing market is dividing. Affordability is expected to stabilize between 2026-2028 due to rising incomes. However, luxury and premium home sales are soaring with new supply, while the affordable housing sector (under ₹45 lakh) remains stressed by high costs and policy issues.

item.title: Middle East Conflict Lifts India Energy Stocks, Signals Higher Oil Prices
item.description: The Middle East conflict is changing global energy markets, pointing to a 'new normal' of higher crude oil and LNG prices. CLSA reports that Indian upstream producers like ONGC and Oil India stand to gain from this shift, with prices potentially settling 10-20% above pre-war levels. However, oil marketing companies face pressure on their profit margins from higher input costs. Gas-focused firms might see delayed investor interest, though some specific areas offer opportunities. While markets expect a quick rally if tensions ease, long-term supply limits and a greater focus on energy security are altering how investors view the sector.

item.title: GR Infra Sells 3 Subsidiaries for ₹273 Crore, Exits Key Highways
item.description: GR Infraprojects Ltd is selling its entire stake in three subsidiaries—GR Bilaspur Urga Highway, GR Ujjain Badnawar Highway, and GR Ena Kim Expressway—for a total of ₹273.22 crore. The deal includes GEKEPL, a material subsidiary. This move comes as GR Infra's stock has seen year-to-date declines.

item.title: Poggipolini Buys Stake in India's Aero Fasteners to Boost Defense Production
item.description: Italian aerospace engineering firm Poggipolini S.p.A. has acquired a majority stake in India's Aero Fasteners Private Limited. This strategic move expands Poggipolini into India's growing defense and aerospace sector, aiming to enhance local design and manufacturing of advanced engineered products by combining Poggipolini's global expertise with Aero Fasteners' established presence. Legal advisors Khaitan & Co and Emerald Law Offices facilitated the cross-border deal.

item.title: Bluspring Acquires Steag India to Boost Power Services, Expand Global Reach
item.description: Bluspring Enterprises, a Fairfax-backed infrastructure firm, has acquired Steag Energy Services (India) Private Limited through its subsidiary Bluspring New Horizon One Private Limited. The deal integrates Steag's India business, enhancing Bluspring's upstream and downstream power service capabilities and driving expansion both at home and abroad.

item.title: Aerchain Secures $13M Series A to Boost AI Procurement Platform
item.description: Bengaluru-based Aerchain has raised $13 million in a Series A funding round, led by Pavestone Technology Fund with IndiaMART participating. The funds will accelerate global expansion and boost AI capabilities for Aerchain's 'Spend Operating System,' which uses AI agents to automate procurement. The platform aims to shift procurement from a cost center to a strategic driver, tapping into the growing trend of AI in business spending.

item.title: India Industries Face 800% Cost Surge Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: Middle East tensions are severely disrupting Indian industries. Logistics costs have soared by up to 800%, and energy supplies are threatened. The crisis worsens liquidity for MSMEs and reveals India's vulnerability from import reliance. This calls for stronger domestic resilience and diverse supply chains to fight ongoing inflation and economic instability.

item.title: Fed Caution Challenges Gold, Silver Rally Fueled by Geopolitics
item.description: Gold and silver prices are climbing amid global tensions and a weaker dollar. However, the Federal Reserve's careful stance on interest rates and a potentially stronger dollar present major challenges. Analysts are split on future price targets, citing the complex mix of global events and economic factors affecting the metals. New Indian regulations for gold and silver funds also add a new dynamic.

item.title: Gold Holds Steady as US-Iran Diplomacy Falters Over Troop Buildup
item.description: Gold prices paused near $4,520 an ounce after a slight two-day recovery. Traders are watching mixed messages from the US and Iran about peace talks. Despite hopeful signs, US troop movements in the Middle East are raising concerns about potential conflict escalation.

item.title: Arm Holdings Surges on AI Demand, On Holding Falls After CEO Exit
item.description: Global markets are reacting sharply to rising Middle East tensions and surging oil prices. Asian stocks mostly fell. However, tech company Arm Holdings gained analyst upgrades, boosted by AI demand. Meanwhile, sportswear firm On Holding's shares dropped after its CEO unexpectedly resigned, showing leadership transition risks. Gold prices were mixed.

item.title: Aluminium Prices Surge: India's Big Producers Cheer, Small Firms Struggle Under Policy
item.description: Global aluminium prices have surged due to West Asian geopolitical tensions, creating a major opportunity for large Indian producers such as Hindalco, Vedanta, and NALCO. However, this price hike severely threatens India's 3,500 aluminium-processing MSMEs. These smaller companies face rising raw material costs because of rigid import duties and falling domestic demand. This puts nearly a million jobs and India's manufacturing goals at risk. The current policy seems to favour large, capital-intensive companies over the job-rich downstream sector, creating a significant economic conflict.

item.title: Bhutan Sells $150M Bitcoin, Raising Gelephu Project Funding Concerns
item.description: Bhutan's government is rapidly selling off Bitcoin, moving over $150 million this year. This sharp reduction in digital assets raises concerns about funding for its major Gelephu Mindfulness City project.

item.title: India Now Protects GUI Designs: Landmark Court Ruling Boosts Digital IP
item.description: The Calcutta High Court has ruled that Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs) can now be registered as designs in India. This significant decision overturns years of rejection by the Designs Office, bringing India's intellectual property protection for digital interfaces closer to international standards. It empowers tech firms and startups to secure the visual elements of their digital products, potentially influencing investment and competition in the digital economy.

item.title: Infosys Expands AI, Cloud with Healthcare & Insurance Tech Buys
item.description: Infosys significantly expanded its strategic capabilities in late March 2026. The company secured global AI recognition and approved two major U.S. acquisitions: Optimum Healthcare IT for $465 million and Stratus Global LLC for $95 million, aiming to strengthen its foothold in healthcare and insurance. Infosys also extended its partnership with the University of Nottingham to modernize its student management system using AI and cloud platforms. Infosys stock closed slightly higher at Rs 1,279.10 on March 25, 2026, with heavy trading volume.

item.title: Nifty Hits 23,500 Hurdle as Sectors Surge Amid Geopolitical Risks
item.description: India's Nifty 50 index recovered strongly, closing at 23,306 after a gap-up start and intraday highs above 23,465. Profit-taking cut gains, but Realty and PSU Banks sectors soared over 2.5%. However, the Nifty's struggle to break the 23,500 resistance level signals caution due to geopolitical developments and oil price swings. Clearing 23,500 is key for further gains, with support near 23,000.

item.title: India's Booming Office Market Overwhelms Infrastructure, Fuels Cyber Risk
item.description: India's office market is setting leasing records, driven by Global Capability Centres (GCCs) using AI. But this boom stresses building infrastructure, which struggles with the high-capacity digital connectivity needed. Network issues and cyber vulnerabilities in smart buildings create risks for business continuity and property value. The recent acquisition of WiredScore by Meter signals a growing focus on crucial digital foundations.

item.title: ICICI Bank: India's FY27 Inflation Forecast Hiked to 4.5% on Energy Prices
item.description: ICICI Bank now expects India's retail inflation to hit 4.5% in fiscal year 2027, up from an earlier prediction. This rise is due to higher energy costs and a major change in how the Consumer Price Index (CPI) is calculated. The updated CPI basket gives more weight to energy, making India's economy more vulnerable to global oil price swings. Each $10 rise in crude oil could add 0.5% to 0.6% to CPI. This contrasts with a forecast of low inflation for FY26.

item.title: Kotak Bank Faces Scrutiny Over Rs 150 Cr FD Fraud After Ex-Employee Arrest
item.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank is working to reconcile about ₹150 crore in fixed deposits tied to the Municipal Corporation of Panchkula amid fraud allegations. The arrest of a former bank manager by Haryana Police, accused of submitting fake deposit reports, has put the bank's internal controls and operations under a spotlight. Kotak Mahindra Bank has filed its own police complaint.

item.title: Bitcoin stalls below $72K despite record low supply and ETF inflows
item.description: Bitcoin is trading near $70,686, facing resistance around $71,500-$72,000 despite exchange supply hitting a seven-year low. The upcoming launch of Morgan Stanley's spot Bitcoin ETF signals increasing institutional adoption. However, cautious sentiment prevails, driven by inflation worries and global uncertainty. Traders anticipate higher volatility as these forces collide, awaiting a clear catalyst for the next price direction.

item.title: NCLT Denies Auditors Immunity in IL&FS Case, Raising EY, Deloitte Legal Risk
item.description: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has ruled that auditors, including Deloitte and EY India (which absorbed BSR & Associates and SRBC & Co), cannot claim blanket immunity in the IL&FS fraud case. The tribunal rejected their requests to be excluded and will now examine their conduct case-by-case, potentially increasing litigation exposure for major audit firms.

item.title: Iran Reportedly Adds Strait of Hormuz Toll; India Denies Levy Claims
item.description: Reports suggest Iran's IRGC is imposing a de facto toll system on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, requiring documentation and escorted passage. However, India's Shipping Ministry has dismissed these claims as baseless, emphasizing international conventions that guarantee freedom of navigation. This development comes amidst heightened regional tensions, impacting critical global trade routes.

item.title: India Markets Closed for Holiday Amid Geopolitical Warnings
item.description: Indian markets are closed on March 26, 2026, for Ram Navami. This follows Wednesday's rally, boosted by hopes of de-escalation in West Asia and lower oil prices. However, ongoing geopolitical tensions are causing analysts to lower forecasts, citing risks of currency depreciation and slower growth.

item.title: End-of-Year Tax Savings: Harvest Losses or Lock Gains by March 31
item.description: As the fiscal year nears its end, investors can strategically sell investments for tax losses or lock in tax-free gains to significantly lower their tax bill. Selling assets that have dropped in value realizes losses that can offset capital gains. Meanwhile, booking gains up to ₹1.25 lakh is tax-free. This strategy is key amid market ups and downs but requires balancing tax benefits with long-term goals. Unused losses can be carried forward for eight years.

item.title: Infosys Buys Healthcare AI Firms for $560M to Boost Digital Services
item.description: Infosys is acquiring Optimum Healthcare IT for $465 million and Stratus Global LLC for $95 million, a combined $560 million deal. The acquisitions aim to strengthen Infosys's digital transformation services in healthcare and insurance by integrating the new expertise with its Infosys Topaz AI and Infosys Cobalt cloud platforms. This expansion is set to accelerate AI-driven cloud and data projects for clients in these sectors, improving operational efficiency and customer experiences.

item.title: India Markets Tested as New Global Risks Emerge
item.description: Indian markets have often shown resilience after global crises, rewarding patient investors with strong rebounds. While past events led to significant gains, recent geopolitical tensions have caused substantial wealth loss of about ₹37 lakh crore. Today's situation involves high import dependence and changing global forces, meaning past recovery patterns might face new challenges.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp Steel Sale to Jindal Stalls on Pension Debt, Energy Costs
item.description: Thyssenkrupp's plan to sell its steel business, Thyssenkrupp Steel Europe (TKSE), is hitting major obstacles. Talks with Jindal Steel International are reportedly stalled over how much the unit is worth. Big issues include TKSE's €2.4 billion in pension debt and higher European energy prices, worsened by the Iran conflict. This stall puts Thyssenkrupp CEO Miguel Lopez's strategy to simplify the company into a holding structure at risk, possibly leading to a costly sale or keeping the unit. Experts have differing opinions, citing potential value but also risks in making the deal happen.

item.title: Malabar Gold Pushes ₹5,300 Crore Expansion Amid Market Jitters
item.description: Malabar Gold & Diamonds is embarking on a significant expansion, earmarking ₹5,200–5,300 crore for capital expenditure to open approximately 50 new showrooms in FY27, adding to its 400+ global locations. This ambitious push occurs against a backdrop of geopolitical tensions impacting gold prices and consumer sentiment. The company is pivoting its strategy towards "design-led affordability" and promoting 18-carat gold to attract value-conscious consumers, seeking to mitigate risks associated with market volatility and shifting demand patterns. A substantial CSR commitment of ₹200 crore further supports its corporate image.

item.title: India Gets Iranian LPG Under US Waiver Amid Supply Crisis
item.description: India has received its first liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cargo from Iran in years, secured under a historical 30-day US sanctions waiver. This move addresses a severe domestic gas supply crisis, forcing industrial allocation cuts. The tanker 'Aurora' is headed for Mangalore, with payment reportedly in Indian rupees. The acquisition highlights India's vulnerability in energy sourcing amidst geopolitical tensions and its efforts to circumvent dollar-based sanctions. State-run firms Indian Oil, Bharat Petroleum, and Hindustan Petroleum will distribute the supply.

item.title: Dhurandhar Franchise Tops ₹2200 Crore, Shifting Bollywood Focus to Directors
item.description: The Dhurandhar franchise has achieved a massive ₹2200+ crore worldwide box office, highlighting a growing trend where directorial vision and authentic stories are gaining ground over traditional star power. This success is prompting a rethink of filmmaking strategies and audience engagement in the Hindi film industry.

item.title: Auto Stocks: Pricing Power Shields Select Carmakers From Sector Slump
item.description: The auto sector has seen a 15% drop due to Middle East supply chain issues and rising costs, but CLSA still rates select manufacturers 'Outperform'. The firm points to companies like Mahindra & Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, TVS Motor, and Tata Motors' India PV business, which have strong pricing power in premium segments and are best placed to handle these challenges. Although earnings forecasts are down, CLSA believes current prices offer good entry points for these strong players, as the long-term effect on valuations appears minor.

item.title: India Infra Projects Hit by ₹5.66 Lakh Crore Cost Overruns
item.description: India's main infrastructure projects have seen costs rise by ₹5.66 lakh crore by February 2026. The total revised budget for 1,948 projects is now ₹41.98 lakh crore, up from ₹36.32 lakh crore. Despite steady spending and good physical progress on many projects, these rising costs point to ongoing problems with project management and finances, affecting national development.

item.title: India Pharma Price Cap: 0.64% Rise Offers Little Relief Amid Cost Surge
item.description: India's National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA) has approved a 0.64% price increase for scheduled medicines, effective March 2026. The rise, tied to the 2025 Wholesale Price Index (WPI), offers little relief to drugmakers facing significant increases in input costs. This limits the impact of price controls on essential medicine affordability.

item.title: FCC Router Ban Forces Netgear, Cisco to Rethink Supply Chains
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) has banned new foreign-made consumer internet routers, citing national security risks. This move is set to disrupt the U.S. market, affecting major players like Netgear, Cisco Systems, and Asustek Computer. Companies must adjust their supply chains and consumers may face higher prices. The ban stems from ongoing worries about network hardware vulnerabilities and state-sponsored cyber threats.

item.title: Gold Tumbles as Mideast Conflict Pushes Oil Past $100, Dollar Higher
item.description: Asian markets showed mixed performance Thursday as escalating Mideast tensions pushed oil past $100 and strengthened the dollar. Markets are pricing out Fed rate cuts for 2026, while the ECB signaled possible rate hikes due to high inflation. Gold's appeal as a safe haven weakened, with a sharp monthly drop even as Mideast tensions rose. Japan's Nikkei and South Korea's KOSPI saw strength recently, differing from wider regional weakness.

item.title: Vedanta's $5B India Shale Push: Energy Security vs. Risk
item.description: Vedanta's Cairn Oil & Gas is embarking on an aggressive $5 billion expansion to quadruple India's daily oil output to 1 million barrels over a decade, leveraging US shale expertise. This strategic push aims to bolster India's energy security amidst high import dependence. However, the plan confronts substantial execution risks, including India's nascent shale sector, capital intensity, and technical replication challenges.

item.title: Infosys Buys Healthcare IT Firm for $465M to Boost Digital Services
item.description: Infosys plans to buy Optimum Healthcare IT for up to $465 million. This deal will boost Infosys's skills in healthcare consulting and digital services for hospitals. It aims to deepen the company's expertise in electronic health record (EHR) systems. The acquisition signals Infosys's focus on building its healthcare sector offerings, combining its AI and cloud tech with Optimum's deep knowledge. Key challenges will be integrating the companies smoothly and expanding sales to drive growth.

item.title: SIP vs. FD: 10-Year Returns for ₹10k Monthly. PPF Benefits.
item.description: A decade-long comparison pits equity SIPs against Fixed Deposits for Rs 10,000 monthly investors. SIPs project ₹23.23 lakh but with market risk, while 7% FDs yield a near-identical ₹24.01 lakh for guaranteed returns. PPF offers substantial tax-free growth over its 15-year term, projecting ₹32.54 lakh.
